title: sys_rand_get() fallback may not be CSPRNG
description:
In generate_eui64() at lora_l2.c:99-101, sys_rand_get() is used as fallback when hardware ID is unavailable:

    sys_rand_get(eui64, 8);

Zephyr's sys_rand_get() is only cryptographically secure if CONFIG_CSPRNG_ENABLED is set. Without this config, it may use a weak PRNG (xoroshiro128+ or hardware RNG without conditioning).

Impact: On boards without hwinfo and without CONFIG_CSPRNG_ENABLED, the EUI-64 would be derived from a weak random source. While EUI-64 is not a secret, predictable addresses enable address prediction attacks and could affect protocols that use EUI-64 for key derivation.

Fix: Either:
1. Add a Kconfig dependency: select ENTROPY_GENERATOR or CSPRNG_ENABLED when LICHEN_LORA_L2 is enabled, OR
2. Document at the call site that this fallback requires CONFIG_CSPRNG_ENABLED, with a build-time check

Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.c:100
close_reason: Addressed by .10: sys_rand_get() fallback paths were removed. The function now returns -ENODEV when hwinfo is unavailable and -EINVAL when SHA-256 fails, refusing to start rather than using weak RNG.
